name: COSMIAC (Configurable Space Microsystems, Innovations and Application Center)
relevance: A Tier-2 research center at the UNM School of Engineering focusing on space exploration, microsystems, and aerospace innovation.

name: Lobo Launch
relevance: UNM's official student rocketry team that designs and launches high-altitude rockets for competition and research.

name: Institute for Space and Nuclear Power Studies (ISNPS)
relevance: Focuses on the development of space nuclear power and propulsion technologies to enable deep space exploration.

name: Sustainable Space Research Grand Challenge (SSRGC)
relevance: A university-wide initiative to unite space expertise across campuses and expand New Mexico's space economy and science.

name: UNM Robotics Society
relevance: A student organization dedicated to building robots and teaching robotics skills through workshops and competitions.

name: UNMxAFRL Agile Manufacturing Laboratory
relevance: Specializes in foundational robotics research with focus areas in In-Space Servicing, Assembly, and Manufacturing (ISAM).

name: AIAA UNM Student Chapter
relevance: The student chapter of the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics, supporting professional development in aerospace.

name: UNM CHILI HOUSE Team (NASA MINDS)
relevance: A team of UNM researchers that won the NASA MINDS Challenge for designing autonomous systems to grow crops on Mars.

name: UNM Swarmathon Team
relevance: Developed autonomous robotic swarms for the NASA Swarmathon competition to advance resource collection on Mars and the Moon.

name: NASA Space Robotics Challenge Team
relevance: A UNM computer science team that competed in the NASA Space Robotics Challenge to advance autonomous lunar mission software.

name: Applied Pulsed Energy Research Lab (APERL)
relevance: Conducts research on pulsed power, high power microwaves, and plasma physics with applications in directed energy and space systems.

name: Directed Energy Center
relevance: An AFRL-UNM partnership focusing on directed energy research, including plasma and microwave technologies.

name: Precision Quantum Sensing Group
relevance: Develops quantum sensors for high-precision metrology and sensing with potential space-based applications.

name: Barron Research Group
relevance: Develops instrumentation for cosmic microwave background (CMB) measurements and other astrophysical observations.

name: CREATE (Center for Rapid Environmental Assessment and Terrain Evaluation)
relevance: Uses remote sensing and GIS technologies for rapid environmental assessment and infrastructure monitoring.

name: ARTS Lab
relevance: Received NASA funding to support STEAM programs like the Artemis in 3D Challenge and provide community access to space tech.

name: New Mexico Space Grant Consortium (NMSGC) - UNM Branch
relevance: A NASA-funded consortium that supports space research, education, and outreach programs at UNM.

name: NM NASA EPSCoR - UNM Projects
relevance: Funds competitive space-related research and technology development projects at UNM through NASA EPSCoR grants.

name: UNM-Gallup SkillsUSA Robotics Team
relevance: UNM-Gallup students compete in the SkillsUSA Robotics and Automation Technology competitions at state and national levels.

name: UNM-Los Alamos Robotics Program
relevance: Provides students at the UNM Los Alamos campus with opportunities to design and build autonomous robotic systems.

name: MARHES (Multi-Agent, Robotics, and Heterogeneous Systems) Lab
relevance: Conducts research on mobile sensor networks and cooperative control of unmanned vehicles and robotic swarms.

name: LOBOS (Low Orbit Ballooning Outreach and Science)
relevance: A student-led program for launching high-altitude balloons to conduct atmospheric research and space-related outreach.

name: UNM CubeSat Project
relevance: A student and faculty initiative focused on the design, build, and launch of small satellites (CubeSats) for research.

name: Space Systems Engineering Graduate Program
relevance: A specialized Master's degree program at UNM focused on space systems engineering and spacecraft design.

name: GeoAIR Lab
relevance: Researches geospatial artificial intelligence and remote sensing to solve environmental and societal challenges.

name: SMILab (Smart Management of Infrastructure Laboratory)
relevance: Develops advanced sensing technologies and robotics for monitoring and managing infrastructure systems.
